decoder.h(3) Library Functions Manual decoder.h(3)

NAME

decoder.h - HEVC/H.265 decoder for ASCII art frames using libde265.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define H265_DECODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME 0x01
#define H265_DECODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE 0x02

Functions


h265_decoder_t * h265_decoder_create (void)
void h265_decoder_destroy (h265_decoder_t *decoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_decode (h265_decoder_t *decoder, const uint8_t *encoded_packet, size_t packet_size, uint16_t *output_width, uint16_t *output_height, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_decoder_get_stats (h265_decoder_t *decoder, uint64_t *total_frames, uint64_t *keyframes, uint16_t *last_width, uint16_t *last_height)

Detailed Description

HEVC/H.265 decoder for ASCII art frames using libde265.

Decodes x265-encoded ASCII art frames back into terminal grid data. Automatically reconfigures when frame size changes.

FRAME SIZE CHANGES: The decoder reads width/height from each packet and reconfigures as needed. No explicit API call required - handled transparently during decode.

PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height: u16][encoded_data: x265]

Definition in file decoder.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define H265_DECODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME 0x01

Decoder flags (from packet)

Definition at line 28 of file decoder.h.

#define H265_DECODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE 0x02

Definition at line 29 of file decoder.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t h265_decode (h265_decoder_t * decoder, const uint8_t * encoded_packet, size_t packet_size, uint16_t * output_width, uint16_t * output_height, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t * output_size)

Decode an x265-encoded ASCII frame

The decoder reads width/height from the packet and automatically reconfigures when frame dimensions change.

Parameters

decoder Decoder handle
encoded_packet Encoded packet (flags+width+height+x265_data)
packet_size Size of encoded packet
output_width Output: decoded frame width
output_height Output: decoded frame height
output_buf Buffer for decoded ASCII data (should be at least width×height)
output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual decoded size

Returns

Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success)

INPUT PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height: u16][x265_data...]

Call this as: uint16_t width, height; uint8_t ascii_grid[256*64]; size_t decoded_size = sizeof(ascii_grid); h265_decode(decoder, packet, packet_size, &width, &height,
ascii_grid, &decoded_size);

Definition at line 105 of file decoder.c.

113   {
114     if (!decoder || !encoded_packet || !output_width || !output_height || !output_buf || !output_size) {
115         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid decoder arguments");
116     }
117 
118     if (packet_size < 5) {
119         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Packet too small (minimum 5 bytes)");
120     }
121 
122     // Parse header
123     uint8_t flags = encoded_packet[0];
124     uint16_t width = ((uint16_t)encoded_packet[1] << 8) | encoded_packet[2];
125     uint16_t height = ((uint16_t)encoded_packet[3] << 8) | encoded_packet[4];
126 
127     if (width == 0 || height == 0) {
128         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid frame dimensions: %ux%u", width, height);
129     }
130 
131     size_t required_output = width * height;
132     if (*output_size < required_output) {
133         *output_size = required_output;
134         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu",
135                         required_output, *output_size);
136     }
137 
138     // Decode HEVC data
139     if (packet_size > 5) {
140         const uint8_t *hevc_data = encoded_packet + 5;
141         size_t hevc_size = packet_size - 5;
142 
143         // Send packet to decoder
144         av_packet_from_data(decoder->packet, (uint8_t *)hevc_data, hevc_size);
145 
146         if (avcodec_send_packet(decoder->codec_ctx, decoder->packet) < 0) {
147             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to send packet to decoder");
148         }
149 
150         if (avcodec_receive_frame(decoder->codec_ctx, decoder->frame) < 0) {
151             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to receive decoded frame");
152         }
153     } else {
154         // Empty packet - just signal a frame boundary
155         if (avcodec_send_packet(decoder->codec_ctx, NULL) < 0) {
156             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to flush decoder");
157         }
158     }
159 
160     // Extract Y plane (grayscale intensity)
161     uint8_t *y_plane = decoder->frame->data[0];
162     int y_stride = decoder->frame->linesize[0];
163     int img_width = decoder->frame->width;
164     int img_height = decoder->frame->height;
165 
166     if (img_width != (int)width || img_height != (int)height) {
167         log_warn("Image dimensions mismatch: expected %ux%u, got %dx%d", width, height, img_width, img_height);
168     }
169 
170     // Copy Y plane to output (ASCII intensity values)
171     for (int y = 0; y < img_height && y < (int)height; y++) {
172         memcpy(output_buf + y * width, y_plane + y * y_stride, width);
173     }
174 
175     *output_width = width;
176     *output_height = height;
177     *output_size = width * height;
178 
179     decoder->last_width = width;
180     decoder->last_height = height;
181     decoder->total_frames++;
182 
183     if (flags & H265_DECODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME) {
184         decoder->keyframes++;
185     }
186 
187     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
188 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, h265_decoder::codec_ctx,
    ERROR_INTERNAL, ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE,
    h265_decoder::frame, H265_DECODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME,
    h265_decoder::keyframes, h265_decoder::last_height,
    h265_decoder::last_width, log_warn,
    h265_decoder::packet, SET_ERRNO, and
    h265_decoder::total_frames.


Referenced by h265_server_decode_and_convert().






h265_decoder_t * h265_decoder_create (void )


Create a new x265 decoder for ASCII frames




Returns


Decoder handle, or NULL on error


Definition at line 31 of file decoder.c.


31                                           {
32     h265_decoder_t *dec = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(h265_decoder_t), h265_decoder_t *);
33     if (!dec) {
34         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate decoder structure");
35         return NULL;
36     }
37 
38     // Find HEVC decoder
39     const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_decoder(AV_CODEC_ID_HEVC);
40     if (!codec) {
41         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "HEVC decoder not found");
42         SAFE_FREE(dec);
43         return NULL;
44     }
45 
46     dec->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
47     if (!dec->codec_ctx) {
48         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate codec context");
49         SAFE_FREE(dec);
50         return NULL;
51     }
52 
53     // Open decoder (HEVC decoder doesn't write to stderr like x265 encoder,
54     // but avoid LOG_IO here to prevent process-global stderr redirection)
55     int codec_open_result = avcodec_open2(dec->codec_ctx, codec, NULL);
56 
57     if (codec_open_result < 0) {
58         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to open HEVC decoder");
59         avcodec_free_context(&dec->codec_ctx);
60         SAFE_FREE(dec);
61         return NULL;
62     }
63 
64     // Allocate frame and packet
65     dec->frame = av_frame_alloc();
66     dec->packet = av_packet_alloc();
67     dec->parser = av_parser_init(AV_CODEC_ID_HEVC);
68 
69     if (!dec->frame || !dec->packet || !dec->parser) {
70         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame/packet/parser");
71         if (dec->frame) av_frame_free(&dec->frame);
72         if (dec->packet) av_packet_free(&dec->packet);
73         if (dec->parser) av_parser_close(dec->parser);
74         avcodec_free_context(&dec->codec_ctx);
75         SAFE_FREE(dec);
76         return NULL;
77     }
78 
79     log_info("FFmpeg HEVC decoder created");
80 
81     // Register decoder for debugging
82     NAMED_REGISTER_H265_DECODER(dec, "h265_decoder", NULL);
83 
84     return dec;
85 }

    ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_MEMORY, h265_decoder::frame,
    log_info, NAMED_REGISTER_H265_DECODER,
    h265_decoder::packet, h265_decoder::parser,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, and SET_ERRNO.

89

  total_frames, uint64_t * keyframes, uint16_t * last_width,
  uint16_t * last_height)


Get decoder statistics




Parameters


decoder Decoder handle


total_frames Output: total frames decoded


keyframes Output: total keyframes decoded


last_width Output: width of last decoded frame


last_height Output: height of last decoded frame
